introSection:
Speed - The Ride was a Premier Rides launched shuttle roller coaster previously located at the Nascar Café at Sahara Hotel & Casino in Las Vegas, Nevada, USA. Opened in 2000, it was known for being the casino's star attraction. A portion of the ride ran through the interior of the casino building including the loading area. It sat on the Las Vegas Strip, which was intercepted by Sahara Avenue and Las Vegas Boulevard. The ride closed on May 1, 2011 and is currently lying in storage.

The ride was designed and manufactured by Premier Rides and fabricated by Intermountain Lift.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
In 1999, Sahara Hotel & Casino announced that Nascar Café would open in 2000 as part of a renovation. The Nascar Café would feature a restaurant, an arcade, and a Premier Rides Shuttle Catapult Coaster named Speed - The Ride. The ride was scheduled to open on March 2, 2000 along with Nascar Café, but its opening was delayed to April 28, 2000. Upon its opening, it was the tallest and fastest roller coaster in Las Vegas. It was also the first Premier Rides coaster to include a vertical loop.

In August 2008, the ride temporarily closed with no information shared from the venue. The ride reopened in February 2009.

On March 11, 2011, it was announced that Sahara Hotel & Casino would close on May 16, 2011. Speed - The Ride operated for the last time on May 1. Dismantling of the coaster began in April 2012.

According to the contractor dismantling the ride, Speed was set to reopen at Akita Plaza, a small shopping center across the street from Mandalay Bay.

By 2019, a portion of the ride was reportedly recycled for scrap. However, the attraction remains for sale on used rides website Amusement-Rides.com. As of 2024, pieces of the coaster remained in storage.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Although the station was located inside the building while the layout ran outside, the transfer track remained outdoors with a large door that opened when it was needed.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: Speed - The Ride started with an LIM launch that accelerated the train from 0- in 2.0 seconds. The train traveled outside and passed through an over-banked curve over the walkway and dove through a tunnel underneath. It then traveled through a tall vertical loop. The train then headed into an LIM boost that accelerated from - in 2.0 seconds while running past the tall Sahara archway. Riders traveled through an s-curve near the casino entrance before launching up a tall vertical rollback. The train decelerated as it ascended the vertical rollback and then navigated the course again in reverse.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Speed - The Ride had a blue track and tan supports.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Single train with 6 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 24 riders

fullText:
Speed - The Ride was a Premier Rides launched shuttle roller coaster previously located at the Nascar Café at Sahara Hotel & Casino in Las Vegas, Nevada, USA. Opened in 2000, it was known for being the casino's star attraction. A portion of the ride ran through the interior of the casino building including the loading area. It sat on the Las Vegas Strip, which was intercepted by Sahara Avenue and Las Vegas Boulevard. The ride closed on May 1, 2011 and is currently lying in storage.

The ride was designed and manufactured by Premier Rides and fabricated by Intermountain Lift.

In 1999, Sahara Hotel & Casino announced that Nascar Café would open in 2000 as part of a renovation. The Nascar Café would feature a restaurant, an arcade, and a Premier Rides Shuttle Catapult Coaster named Speed - The Ride. The ride was scheduled to open on March 2, 2000 along with Nascar Café, but its opening was delayed to April 28, 2000. Upon its opening, it was the tallest and fastest roller coaster in Las Vegas. It was also the first Premier Rides coaster to include a vertical loop.

In August 2008, the ride temporarily closed with no information shared from the venue. The ride reopened in February 2009.

On March 11, 2011, it was announced that Sahara Hotel & Casino would close on May 16, 2011. Speed - The Ride operated for the last time on May 1. Dismantling of the coaster began in April 2012.

According to the contractor dismantling the ride, Speed was set to reopen at Akita Plaza, a small shopping center across the street from Mandalay Bay.

By 2019, a portion of the ride was reportedly recycled for scrap. However, the attraction remains for sale on used rides website Amusement-Rides.com. As of 2024, pieces of the coaster remained in storage.



Although the station was located inside the building while the layout ran outside, the transfer track remained outdoors with a large door that opened when it was needed.

Speed - The Ride started with an LIM launch that accelerated the train from 0- in 2.0 seconds. The train traveled outside and passed through an over-banked curve over the walkway and dove through a tunnel underneath. It then traveled through a tall vertical loop. The train then headed into an LIM boost that accelerated from - in 2.0 seconds while running past the tall Sahara archway. Riders traveled through an s-curve near the casino entrance before launching up a tall vertical rollback. The train decelerated as it ascended the vertical rollback and then navigated the course again in reverse.

Speed - The Ride had a blue track and tan supports.

Single train with 6 cars. Riders are arranged 2 across in 2 rows for a total of 24 riders




 * Speed-The Ride Re-opening
